function.name: Read
function.description: Read the complete contents of a file. Accepts absolute paths (starting with /) or relative paths from current directory. Returns the raw text content. Large files are truncated - use offset to read specific portions.

filePath.description: Path to the file - either absolute (e.g., /etc/hosts) or relative (e.g., ./README.md)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Useful for reading large files in chunks. Defaults to 1.

function.name: Glob
function.description: Find files matching a glob pattern from the current directory. Use * to match any characters within a name, ** to match across directories. Returns list of matching file paths. Output truncated after 500 lines; use more specific patterns for larger result sets.

function.name: Grep
function.description: Search for lines matching a pattern in files. Use regex patterns to find text across one or more files. Output truncated after 500 lines; refine your pattern or glob to narrow results.

function.name: Bash
function.description: Execute a shell command and return output. Dangerous commands are blocked. Output truncated after 500 lines; use filters to narrow output.

function.name: Write
function.description: Write or create a file with the given content. Creates parent directories if needed.

path.description: Path to the file to write (absolute or relative)

content.description: The complete content to write to the file

function.name: Edit
function.description: Find and replace text in an existing file. Finds old_string and replaces with new_string. old_string must be unique in the file.

path.description: Path to the file to edit

old_string.description: Exact text to find and replace
